meta_title: For Students: marketing automation to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Marketing Automation for Students: Build Your First AI App
content.seo_description: Break through beginner plateaus. Master marketing automation with Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade AI projects for your portfolio.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the beginner plateau requires moving beyond simple CRUD apps to utility-driven automation. By leveraging Antigravity for orchestration and Claude Code for rapid iterative development, you can build a marketing automation tool that delivers real-world value. This approach transforms your portfolio from 'tutorial-follower' to 'problem-solver' in a single weekend.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks grinding through tutorials, building yet another To-Do list or weather app. You have the syntax, but you lack the architecture. The feeling of being 'stuck' isn't a lack of talent—it's a lack of leverage. You are currently building components, not systems. 

Marketing automation is the bridge between a static script and a professional SaaS product. By automating lead qualification or content distribution, you create a tangible asset that businesses actually pay for. It’s time to stop chasing features and start building engines.

steps.title: Define the Automation Trigger
steps.description: Identify a repetitive task, like scraping social sentiment or drafting email responses. Focus on a single input-to-output pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a lead qualification workflow that accepts email input and outputs a sentiment score and urgency level using a structured JSON format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project structure with typescript and zod schema for lead data'

steps.title: Build the Logic Engine with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define the flow state of your automation, ensuring state persistence between execution steps.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a state machine for a drip campaign that handles three states: lead_received, follow_up_sent, and conversion_confirmed.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'import antigravity-sdk and scaffold the state machine handler'

steps.title: Integrate LLM Logic via Claude Code
steps.description: Inject the reasoning layer to make decisions based on the data processed in your workflow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a prompt template that classifies marketing leads as 'hot', 'warm', or 'cold' based on their email content.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'implement a function that calls the Claude API to analyze lead sentiment using the defined prompt template'

steps.title: Implement the Delivery Layer
steps.description: Connect your automation to an external API like SendGrid or a Slack webhook to execute the action.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular handler for sending Slack notifications when a 'hot' lead is detected in the pipeline.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a webhook listener and integrate Slack notification service'

steps.title: Test and Iterate with Claude
steps.description: Use Claude Code to simulate edge cases and refine the logic to ensure high reliability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of unit tests that simulate malformed email inputs to ensure the pipeline fails gracefully.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'run all unit tests and provide a summary of potential bottlenecks in the automation flow'

pro_tips: Always build your automation as a 'stateless' engine first; it makes debugging significantly easier when you can inspect the state at any point.
Use 'Human-in-the-loop' checkpoints for your first project; don't fully automate high-stakes actions until you've verified the model's accuracy.
Focus on 'Logging-as-a-Feature'; include detailed execution logs in your UI so users understand exactly why the AI made a specific decision.
Limit your scope to one specific niche, like 'Real Estate Lead Scoring', to make your portfolio project stand out to recruiters.
Leverage Claude Code to generate 'self-healing' code blocks that catch common API timeout errors automatically.
content.next_steps: Once your first automation is live, deploy it as a serverless function and integrate a simple frontend to visualize the lead flow for your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: Stop building apps that gather dust and start building systems that drive results. You have the tools, the guide, and the momentum to move from student to engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
